title: Fix config persistence — api-layer and api-m2 tests failing
description:
Two config persistence tests are failing:

1. test/server/api-layer.test.js:52 — PUT /api/config then GET /api/config does not return the saved testKey
2. test/server/api-m2.test.js:83 — config file on disk contains malformed JSON (SyntaxError: Unexpected non-whitespace character after JSON at position 348 line 20 column 2)

Both failures suggest the config write path is producing invalid JSON on disk. Investigate src/server/api.js PUT /api/config handler and the config persistence mechanism. The JSON file is likely being appended to rather than overwritten, or multiple writes are racing.

Error from latest-test.log:
```
FAIL test/server/api-layer.test.js > api-layer > PUT then GET /api/config round-trips
expect(body.testKey).toBe(cfg.testKey)

FAIL test/server/api-m2.test.js > GET /api/config — persistence > config persists on disk as JSON
SyntaxError: Unexpected non-whitespace character after JSON at position 348
```

Fix the config write to produce valid JSON and ensure round-trip works.
